channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: Exciting Indiana Jones Inspired Platformer Lifted Coming From Former Disney Imagineer
item.content:encoded:
Adventure Works, founded by former Disney Imagineer Jeffrey Ashbrook, took to the stage during the MIx Summer Game Showcase to show their highly impressive cinematic puzzle platformer Lifted off, as well as announced a Lifted release date.

Inspired by 80s adventure films, most notably Indiana Jones, Lifted has you playing as student Ari Buktu seeking extra credit from Professor Lionel Raventhorpe. These requests are swiftly parlayed into an adventure far grander than anything Ari could have imagined as they end up going on a time-traveling adventure via the professor's Anytime Elevator as Professor Raventhorpe seeks to find the one thing that he believes he needs: a legendary tiara to ask Miss Barton, another teacher, out on a date.

Comedic and high energy, the platformer  has you traveling in different areas like 19th century Egypt, ancient Mayan civilizations or the 18th century Caribbean. Much like Doctor Who's TARDIS the Elevator isn't necessarily a precision device, and it often puts you in awkward locations, and you need to solve puzzles, pursue hijinks, and discover your way through different problems in a buddy-comedy blockbuster type affair.

"I grew up loving movies and games that made the world feel bigger than my living room. Lifted is the closest I've gotten to making one,” said Jeffrey Ashbrook, Founder of Adventure Works. “I wanted it to be a game kids could love on their own, and that parents could hand to them feeling like they were passing along a little of what they grew up on."

Lifted is set to lift off on PC on July 22nd with Nintendo Switch 2, and PlayStation 5 versions following at a later date. There's a demo currently available on Steam that you can try if you want to see if Lifted is the uplifting experience you want in your life.

A longtime lover of speculative fiction, in almost all its forms, Don joined TechRaptor in 2014 on a whim sending in an application as he was looking for… More about Don
